title: Parts of Speech
learning_outcomes: Describe structural characteristics of Parts of Speech.
Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for Nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, and transition conjunctions, Coordination vs subordination.

content_sections.title: Parts of Speech
content_sections.content:
### Syntactic Alignment, Modifiers & Logical Transition Indicators
Verbal Aptitude studies grammatical structure, modifier bounds, and logical flow connections.

#### Subject-Verb Agreement with Intervening Phrases
Verbs must align with their subjects, not object nouns located in intervening prepositional phrases (e.g. "One of the servers *is* down," not "One of the servers *are* down").

#### Dangling Modifiers
A modifying phrase must refer to an active subject declared immediately after the phrase. If the subject is omitted, the modifier is left dangling:
*   *Dangling (Incorrect):* "While writing code, the system crashed."
*   *Resolved (Correct):* "While I was writing code, the system crashed."

#### Logical Transition categories
*   **Adversative:** Indicates contrast (e.g. *nevertheless*, *nonetheless*, *conversely*).
*   **Causal:** Indicates result (e.g. *therefore*, *hence*, *consequently*).

### Depth Analysis: Parts of Speech
In production configurations, we analyze how Nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, and transition conjunctions, Coordination vs subordination affects performance parameters. We evaluate memory layouts and consensus thresholds.

callouts.title: Common Trap in Parts of Speech
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Parts of Speech is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.

callouts.title: Production Scaling for Parts of Speech
callouts.content: Production servers implement strict resource isolation and cache alignments to prevent performance regressions when running Parts of Speech loops.
